focus: exercises to remember the correct article for each noun and build confidence when choosing between der, die, or das

sections.title: Strategy: How to Tackle Gender in A1 Vocabulary
sections.content:
Since you already understand what der/die/das mean, the challenge is knowing WHICH one applies to each noun. There is no single perfect rule that covers all German nouns — but there are two tools you can combine:

1. PATTERN recognition: certain noun endings and semantic categories reliably predict gender. Learn these patterns and you can make educated guesses for new words.

2. MEMORIZE as a unit: learn every noun with its article from day one. Never write 'Hotel' alone — always write 'das Hotel'. Treat the article as part of the word.

At A1 level, you do NOT need to know every German noun. Focus on the ~100–150 nouns that appear in Momente A1 and travel/tourism contexts. This session drills the most important ones.

sections.title: Reliable Ending Patterns — Your Best Guessing Tool
sections.content:
These patterns are not 100% perfect but cover the majority of A1 vocabulary:

| Ending | Gender | Reliability | Examples |
|--------|--------|-------------|----------|
| -ung | die (f) | very high | die Wohnung, die Zeitung, die Reservierung, die Rechnung |
| -heit / -keit | die (f) | very high | die Freiheit, die Möglichkeit |
| -tion / -sion | die (f) | very high | die Station, die Lektion, die Information |
| -e (abstract/place nouns) | die (f) | high | die Straße, die Sprache, die Apotheke, die Schule, die Küche |
| -er (agent nouns) | der (m) | high | der Lehrer, der Kellner, der Bäcker |
| -ling | der (m) | high | der Frühling |
| Days / months / seasons | der (m) | always | der Montag, der März, der Sommer |
| -chen / -lein | das (n) | always | das Mädchen, das Brötchen |
| -ment | das (n) | very high | das Dokument, das Instrument |
| Infinitive used as noun | das (n) | always | das Essen, das Frühstück |
| -um (borrowed words) | das (n) | high | das Museum, das Datum |

IMPORTANT — exceptions to watch out for:
- der Junge (boy) ends in -e but is masculine
- das Cafe ends in -e but is neuter
- die Butter ends in -er but is feminine
These exceptions are rare at A1; the patterns above cover most cases.

sections.title: High-Frequency A1 Noun Reference by Gender
sections.content:
Memorize these as fixed units (article + noun). They are the backbone of A1 German.

DER (masculine):
der Mann (man) · der Bahnhof (train station) · der Zug (train) · der Bus (bus) · der Flughafen (airport) · der Kaffee (coffee) · der Tee (tea) · der Wein (wine) · der Saft (juice) · der Hunger (hunger) · der Arzt (doctor, m) · der Kellner (waiter) · der Lehrer (teacher, m) · der Name (name) · der Tag (day) · der Morgen (morning) · der Abend (evening) · der Markt (market) · der Tisch (table) · der Koffer (suitcase) · der Pass (passport) · der Schlüssel (key) · der Supermarkt (supermarket) · der Preis (price)

DIE (feminine):
die Frau (woman) · die Stadt (city) · die Straße (street) · die Apotheke (pharmacy) · die Schule (school) · die Uhr (clock/time) · die Sprache (language) · die Wohnung (flat) · die Zeitung (newspaper) · die Küche (kitchen) · die Tasche (bag) · die Reise (trip) · die Rechnung (bill) · die Reservierung (reservation) · die Adresse (address) · die Nummer (number) · die Stunde (hour) · die Nacht (night) · die Woche (week) · die Kirche (church) · die Bäckerei (bakery) · die Information (information)

DAS (neuter):
das Hotel (hotel) · das Restaurant (restaurant) · das Zimmer (room) · das Essen (food/meal) · das Wasser (water) · das Bier (beer) · das Kind (child) · das Buch (book) · das Ticket (ticket) · das Geld (money) · das Auto (car) · das Taxi (taxi) · das Handy (mobile phone) · das Frühstück (breakfast) · das Gepäck (luggage) · das Museum (museum) · das Dokument (document) · das Formular (form) · das Café (café)

transfer_notes: Spanish gender rarely matches German gender for the same concept — do not rely on Spanish as a guide. For example: el sol (m) → die Sonne (f); el agua (m) → das Wasser (n); la ciudad (f) → die Stadt (f) ✓ — some match, many don't.

English gives you no information about German gender since English nouns have no article gender. This is why memorizing der/die/das with each noun is essential — you cannot reconstruct it from English.
The -ung ending is your single most reliable pattern at A1 level: it is almost always feminine (die). If you see an -ung noun you haven't memorized, guess die.
Neuter is often the 'default' for borrowed words from English (das Ticket, das Handy, das Internet, das Taxi). When in doubt about a modern borrowed word, das is a reasonable guess.
explanation.key_takeaway: Learn every A1 noun as a fixed pair (der/die/das + noun), use the -ung → die and -chen/-lein → das patterns to help with unknowns, and when in doubt about borrowed words from English, guess das.

examples.grammar_note: das Hotel — neuter; borrowed word from French/English, das is common for modern borrowed nouns.
der Bahnhof — masculine; must-memorize noun, no reliable ending pattern.

examples.english: The pharmacy is on the right.
examples.grammar_note: die Apotheke — feminine; -e ending on a place noun → usually die.
die Wohnung (feminine, -ung ending) + das Zimmer (neuter, must-memorize).
der Kaffee — masculine; must-memorize, the -ee ending is not a reliable pattern.

examples.german: Die Reservierung ist für morgen Abend.
examples.english: The reservation is for tomorrow evening.
examples.grammar_note: die Reservierung — feminine; -ung ending → always die.

examples.german: Der Zug nach München fährt um acht Uhr ab.
examples.english: The train to Munich departs at eight o'clock.
examples.grammar_note: der Zug (masculine, must-memorize) · die Uhr (feminine, must-memorize).

examples.german: Das Ticket kostet zwanzig Euro und der Pass ist im Koffer.
examples.english: The ticket costs twenty euros and the passport is in the suitcase.
examples.grammar_note: das Ticket (neuter, borrowed word) · der Pass (masculine) · der Koffer (masculine).

examples.german: Die Rechnung bitte — das Restaurant hat ein gutes Frühstück.
examples.english: The bill, please — the restaurant has a good breakfast.
examples.grammar_note: die Rechnung (feminine, -ung) · das Restaurant (neuter, borrowed) · das Frühstück (neuter, compound from infinitive Frühstück).

examples.german: Das Museum ist in der Stadtmitte, neben der Kirche.
examples.english: The museum is in the city centre, next to the church.
examples.grammar_note: das Museum (neuter, -um ending) · die Kirche (feminine, -e ending on a place noun).

examples.german: Der Kellner bringt das Bier und die Rechnung.
examples.english: The waiter brings the beer and the bill.
examples.grammar_note: der Kellner (masculine, -er agent noun) · das Bier (neuter, must-memorize) · die Rechnung (feminine, -ung).

exercises.prompt: ___ Hotel ist sehr gut. (The hotel)
exercises.explanation: das Hotel — neuter. Borrowed word; neuter is common for modern borrowed nouns.

exercises.prompt: ___ Bahnhof ist weit weg. (The train station)
exercises.explanation: der Bahnhof — masculine. Must-memorize; no reliable ending pattern.

exercises.prompt: ___ Wohnung hat drei Zimmer. (The flat)
exercises.explanation: die Wohnung — feminine. The -ung ending is one of the most reliable patterns: always die.

exercises.prompt: ___ Ticket kostet fünfzehn Euro. (The ticket)
exercises.explanation: das Ticket — neuter. Borrowed from English; modern borrowed words are often neuter.

exercises.prompt: ___ Pass und ___ Koffer sind am Bahnhof. (The passport / The suitcase)
exercises.explanation: der Pass and der Koffer are both masculine. Both must be memorized; no ending pattern helps here.

exercises.prompt: ___ Rechnung kommt vom Kellner. ___ Restaurant ist sehr gut. (The bill / The restaurant)
exercises.explanation: die Rechnung (feminine, -ung ending). das Restaurant (neuter, borrowed word).

exercises.prompt:
___ Zug ist pünktlich.
a) Der
b) Die
c) Das
exercises.explanation: der Zug — masculine. Must-memorize; transport nouns are often masculine but there is no rule, so memorize it.

exercises.prompt:
___ Kind schläft.
a) Der
b) Die
c) Das
exercises.explanation: das Kind — neuter. This is a must-memorize noun; despite referring to a child (a person), German uses neuter.

exercises.prompt:
___ Apotheke ist geschlossen.
a) Der
b) Die
c) Das
exercises.explanation: die Apotheke — feminine. The -e ending on a place noun is a reliable indicator of feminine gender.

exercises.prompt:
___ Frühstück ist um sieben Uhr.
a) Der
b) Die
c) Das
exercises.explanation: das Frühstück — neuter. It is a compound noun originally derived from an infinitive (früh + stücken), and nouns made from infinitives are always neuter.

exercises.prompt:
___ Reservierung ist für zwei Nächte.
a) Der
b) Die
c) Das
exercises.explanation: die Reservierung — feminine. The -ung ending is one of the most reliable patterns in German: virtually all -ung nouns are feminine.

exercises.prompt:
___ Museum ist sehr interessant.
a) Der
b) Die
c) Das
exercises.explanation: das Museum — neuter. The -um ending (from Latin/Greek borrowed words) is a reliable indicator of neuter gender.

exercises.prompt: The train station is far away.
exercises.explanation: der Bahnhof — masculine (must-memorize). 'Weit weg' means 'far away'.

exercises.prompt: The pharmacy is on the right.
exercises.explanation: die Apotheke — feminine (-e ending, place noun). 'Rechts' means 'on the right'.

exercises.prompt: The hotel has a restaurant.
exercises.explanation: das Hotel (neuter, borrowed word) · das Restaurant (neuter, borrowed) → 'ein Restaurant' (Akkusativ neuter, no change).

exercises.prompt: The bill, please. The waiter is very friendly.
exercises.solution: Die Rechnung, bitte. Der Kellner ist sehr freundlich.
exercises.explanation: die Rechnung (feminine, -ung ending) · der Kellner (masculine, -er agent noun).

exercises.prompt: Das Mann ist Arzt.
exercises.explanation: der Mann — masculine. Male persons are always masculine in German.

exercises.prompt: Die Hotel ist schön.
exercises.explanation: das Hotel — neuter (not feminine). Borrowed words, especially those ending in a vowel like Hotel, are typically neuter.

exercises.prompt: Der Wohnung hat drei Zimmer.
exercises.explanation: die Wohnung — feminine. The -ung ending always signals feminine gender; it should be die, not der.

exercises.prompt: Das Zug nach Berlin fährt um neun Uhr ab.
exercises.solution: Der Zug nach Berlin fährt um neun Uhr ab.
exercises.explanation: der Zug — masculine (must-memorize). The -ug ending has no pattern rule; this noun must be memorized as masculine.
